---
title: "Ranked Unmined Gold Reserves by Country"
description: "ggplot2 redesign of the gold reserves infographic (Visual Capitalist style)."
---


## Overview

This project is a recreation of the **"Unmined Reserves of Gold"** infographic, below, using **ggplot2**. The original shows estimated gold reserves by country in metric tons and USD value. This redesign focuses on clearly stating the message. 

![Source image: https://elements.visualcapitalist.com/ranked-unmined-gold-reserves-by-country-2025/](../images/GoldDepositFooters.jpg)

## The image
For this project I drew inspiration from my fiancee Emily's home office, where she conducts research on economic markets. As a professional economist she frequently works with data-rich visual resources. Among these, one particularly influential source was [Elements](https://elements.visualcapitalist.com/), a platform dedicated to creating data-driven visuals to bridge the gap between the global trends shaping our future and the raw materials powering them.

![Emily's home office](../images/IMG_1250.jpeg)


### Original
In the original image, **"Ranked: Unmined Gold Reserves by Country"**, the graphic presents a detailed comparison of countries' unmined gold reserves,  ranked by estimated weight. The figure reports reserves in metric tons adn also provides an inferred monetary value for these reserves, calculated using a pegged gold price of $4362 per unit. 

The data source the original image references is credetid to the [U.S Geological Survey Janurary 2025](https://pubs.usgs.gov/periodicals/mcs2025/mcs2025.pdf/). As stated in the reports table of contentx, the section reporting gold is found on page 82 directly after germanium. A table is found on the following page which includes the [data](../data/data.csv) used for this project.

```{R}
"World Mine Production and Reserves: Reserves for Canada, China, Colombia, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, Peru,
Russia, and Tanzania were revised based on company and Government reports. "
```


```{r setup}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(scales)

# Begin by loading the data from the csv file. 
data <- read_csv("../data/data.csv")
# summarise(data)
```



## Data preparation
```{r prep}
#| label: prep
#| echo: true

# Prepare the data, create a filtered set of data from the frame above
```



```{r reserves-plot}
#| label: reserves-plot
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-cap: "Unmined gold reserves by country (metric tons). Value in USD using gold price $4,362/troy oz (U.S. Geological Survey, January 2025)."

# Create the ggplot image adding layers incrementally

```






```{r}
# Include sources for the original images and data below

```

## Source

- **Data / concept:** U.S. Geological Survey, January 2025; value of reserves at gold price $4,362/troy oz.
- **Original infographic:** Visual Capitalist [reference image:](../images/GoldDepositFooters.jpg).
